summary: Implements quickstart CLI command that executes full local lifecycle (init -> send -> inbox -> read -> ack) with deterministic per-step reporting in JSON and human-readable modes. Covers VAL-LAUNCH-007.

strengths: Clear step-by-step lifecycle execution with per-step pass/fail/skip tracking
Failure simulation via --simulate-init-failure supports testing edge cases
Machine-readable JSON output includes totalSteps, passedSteps, remediation array
Human-readable output uses clear ✓/✗/○ markers with next-step guidance
Database handles properly closed in try/finally blocks for each step
Uses execFileSync (argv-based) for init subprocess call - no shell injection risk

issues.description: initCommandSync uses execFileSync to call itself with 'init --json', spawning a subprocess. This works but adds overhead compared to directly calling the async initCommand. Acceptable for a diagnostic one-shot flow.

testCoverage.notes: 349 lines across 18 test cases covering: happy path, step validation, message ID tracking, summary counts, config isolation, idempotency, human-readable output, failure paths with remediation, help integration

nonBlockingIssues.description: quickstart sends to hardcoded 'quickstart-recipient' in local mode which is fine for a diagnostic flow but could confuse users if they check inbox afterwards
